legacyWebHandler.notes: Resolves basketId by first calling GetOrCreateBasketForUser to get BasketViewModel.Id, then builds the quantity dictionary from posted BasketItemViewModel items. The REST target receives basketId directly in the path — no prior basket lookup needed.

entryPoint.signature: Task<Result<Basket>> SetQuantities(int basketId, Dictionary<string, int> quantities)

calls.notes: Spec filters by Baskets.Id and eagerly loads Items via .Include(b => b.Items). Returns null if not found.
If basket == null → returns Result<Basket>.NotFound(). REST target should return 404.

Called for each basket item whose Id appears in the quantities dictionary. Guard.Against.OutOfRange(quantity, 0, int.MaxValue) — negative value throws. Items absent from the dictionary are skipped and left unchanged.
Removes all BasketItem entries with Quantity == 0 from the in-memory collection. EF Core change tracking translates these removals into DELETE statements during UpdateAsync.

db.notes: For items whose quantity was changed to a non-zero value.
For items removed by RemoveEmptyItems() (quantity set to 0).
